Proposed for the Property are eight towers (with heights up to 40 storeys) with associated podiums of approximately six-storeys, four mid-rise buildings, and a two storey community hub building on eight development blocks. To permit a mixed-use development on the Lands, in conformity with the Official Plan designation. The proposal will be completed in two phases with a 46-storey residential tower (Tower 1) located  on the eastern portion of the Lands and a 35-storey residential tower (Tower 2) located on the western portion. An eleven storey base building with retail and daycare uses at grade along The Queensway, and with below grade parking, occupies the entire site. The base building will be connected to the towers with a 4-storey building on both east and west sides of the property. The application for a Zoning By-law Amendment was approved at the Ontario Land Tribunal (OLT) on October 17, 2023 to permit the development and the applicant has resubmitted the application to address conditions of approval. OLT File Number is OLT -21-00179.

A Rezoning Amendment and Draft Plan of Subdivision application to re-develop the site with 6 new residential buildings,  a community centre, a public park, public roads and private driveways.  The buildings would range in height from 4 storeys to 25 storeys and contain a total of 941 new dwelling units consisting of townhouses and apartment units of which 341 are TCHC units and 600 are market units.  The units would consist of townhouses and apartment units.  The existing 152 TCHC apartment units at 5 Needle Firway would be retained.  Building One would contain 123 TCHC units, Building Two would contain 218 TCHC units, Building Three would contain a community centre and daycare facilities, Building Four would contain 283 market units, Building Five would contain 48 market units, Building 7a would contain 142 market units and Building 7b would contain 127 market units.   A total of 667 parking spaces would be provided site wide.Properties include:  3, 36 Marsh Grassway, 7, 11 Blue Grassway, 4, 17 Cane Grassway, 5, 10, 40 Turf Grassway and 1,2, & 8 Dune Grassway.

The building will provide a total of 369 residential units, being a mix of studio, 1-bedroom, 2-bedroom, and 3-bedroom units. A paved access from Talara Dr along the west property line will be provided, leading to 2 levels of below-grade parking, providing a total of 37 parking spaces; as well as 150 bicycle parking spaces. The proposed development provides a total GFA of 30,285.00 square metres and a FSI of 14.75. Additionally, the proposed development provides 862.90 square metres of indoor amenity space and 613.11 square metres of outdoor amenity space.

The proposed development of the subject site comprises a 14-storey, 18,201 square metre, 190-unit residential apartment building. The ground floor of the proposed development contains a series of amenity areas, the building lobby, mail room, moving room, and garbage room. At the rear are accessible parking spaces, lay by areas for pick up/drop off, and short term bicycle parking. Residential units are proposed from floors 2 through 14, inclusive.A total of 10 two- and three-bedroom rental townhouse units are currently located on the subject site. Nine of these units are rented at an affordable level. All 10 units are proposed to be demolished.

A 119-unit 9-storey seniors residence is also proposed abutting a 4,128.6 m2 park fronting onto Evans Avenue. A new public road is also proposed to run through the site at the southwest cornern of the two proposed new public streets. A Draft Plan of Subdivision Application has been submitted for the new public streets and public park.

OLT settlement which contemplates a mixed residential/retail development of five buildings, ranging in height from 17-storeys (with a six-storey mid-rise component) to 49-storeys, with approximately 2,100 dwelling units including a unit mix of 15% two-bedroom units and 10% three-bedroom units. To permit a mixed-use development on the Lands, in conformity with the Official Plan designation. The proposal will be completed in two phases with a 46-storey residential tower (Tower 1) located  on the eastern portion of the Lands and a 35-storey residential tower (Tower 2) located on the western portion. An eleven storey base building with retail and daycare uses at grade along The Queensway, and with below grade parking, occupies the entire site. The base building will be connected to the towers with a 4-storey building on both east and west sides of the property. The application for a Zoning By-law Amendment was approved at the Ontario Land Tribunal (OLT) on October 17, 2023 to permit the development and the applicant has resubmitted the application to address conditions of approval. OLT File Number is OLT -21-00179. This is a resubmission of the Site Plan Control application to align with the resubmitted Zoning By-law Amendment application.
